suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: does_anyone_else
formats.instruction: Start with 'Does anyone else...' or 'Am I the only one who...' — share a relatable experience or pet peeve in 2-3 sentences.

formats.name: til
formats.instruction: Write a brief 'Today I Learned' post — state the surprising fact in one sentence, then 1-2 sentences on why it matters. Keep it tight.

formats.name: storytime
formats.instruction: Tell a longer story with a beginning, middle, and end. Include dialogue if appropriate. Build tension. 200-350 words.

formats.name: guide
formats.instruction: Write a practical beginner's guide to a topic. Structure with clear sections. Be opinionated about what matters and what doesn't. 250-400 words.

formats.name: life_hack
formats.instruction: Share a specific, actionable life hack or tip. Explain the trick in 2-3 sentences. No preamble, no philosophy.

formats.name: numbered_list
formats.instruction: Write a numbered list of 3-5 observations or takes. Each point is 1-2 sentences. No introduction — jump straight into #1.

formats.name: theory
formats.instruction: Present a speculative theory about how something works. Structure: 'I think X because Y. The evidence is Z.' Keep it to one paragraph, 80-120 words.

formats.name: one_liner
formats.instruction: Write a single punchy line — a joke, a truth bomb, or a wild claim. ONE sentence. That's it. Do NOT add explanation.

title_styles: Write a curious, exploratory title. Examples: 'I went down a rabbit hole on X and here's what I found', 'The weirdest thing about X that nobody talks about', 'X is way more interesting than it sounds'
Write a casual, Reddit-style title. Examples: 'TIL that octopuses have three hearts', 'Does anyone else think X is overrated?', 'I just realized something about Y'
Write a storytelling title. Examples: 'The time I accidentally discovered X', 'How a broken Y taught me about Z', 'What happened when I tried X for 30 days'
Write a question title. Examples: 'Why does X happen when Y?', 'Has anyone tried X for Y?', 'What's the actual evidence for X?'
structure_variants: Include a short piece of dialogue or a quote to anchor your point.
Structure this as a story: setup, conflict, resolution (even if brief).
Write it as if you're talking to a friend at a bar — casual, no structure, just vibes.
Write in a single flowing paragraph — no bullet points, no headers, just one continuous thought.
